content: Just as human language learners use lexical reflection to internalize word meanings and correct usage errors, autonomous language agents can combine Reflexion's dynamic memory retention with Self-Refine's iterative feedback to develop a 'linguistic self-internalization' capability that moves far beyond generic output refinement. This hybrid framework lets agents retain lessons from past refinement cycles to avoid repeating the same mistakes, rather than starting from zero on each new self-feedback iteration.
